architecture_fit.note: Single attention layer, no MLP, hand-set W_Q/W_K — minimal delta from base_model; shift-by-k emerges from real Q@K^T softmax and hits mass≈1.0.

baseline_comparison.note: Two strawmen under identical conditions: uniform random_model_fn (~chance 0.031) and the ablated no-shift circuit, both clearly failing where the circuit succeeds.

faithfulness.note: Causal ablation replacing the shift matrix with identity collapses target mass to ~1e-13, proving the offset comes from the W_K shift matrix and not painted-on mass.

operating_range.note: Full canonical offset sweep k∈{1,2,3,4,8} all at 1.0, but the exact hand-built circuit isn't stressed beyond the demo regime (fixed L, no noise/breakpoint shown).

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: The mechanism is fully hand-written (shift matrices in W_K, identity in W_Q) and reproduces the target behaviour exactly — textbook hand-set circuit.

visual_judgement.note: Heatmap with the green i-k sub-diagonal overlay plus grouped circuit/ablation/uniform bar chart make the claim and its falsifier legible at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties y-axis to the exact benchmarked quantity (mass on i-k), x-axis to the full offset sweep, and pairs the artefacts that would break the claim if moved.

notes: A clean, minimal hand-built QK circuit that genuinely computes shift-by-k via softmax(Q@K^T) rather than painting mass on the target, scoring ~1.0 mass and perfect argmax accuracy across the full offset sweep. The causal ablation and uniform strawman provide strong faithfulness and baseline evidence, and the visualisation is well-matched to the benchmarked quantity. Only weakness is operating range: the exact circuit is never pushed to a breaking point beyond the canonical demo regime.
